title: Rootless podman fails to update container some root paths
body:
### Issue Description

When building a container which tries to manipulate the container's rootfs some operations it fail with `Operation not permitted`  i.e. installing mariadb fails to create the `mysql` user but installs the package.

The happens with podman 4.4.x but not with 4.3.x (Tested from Archlinux packages) and I've confirmed with Archlinux packagers that "nothing has changed substantially between the versions in terms of packaging".

Downstream ticket: https://bugs.archlinux.org/task/77825

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

I've prepared a basic Dockefile and script to reproduce this issue. Download from https://pkgbuild.com/~artafinde/podman-test.tgz, untar and build.

1. `curl -O https://pkgbuild.com/~artafinde/podman-test.tgz`
2. `tar zxvf podman-test.tgz`
3. `cd podman-test && podman build .`


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

Container created and some operations fails like below. There are more errors like that later on the logs.
```
[..]
looking for conflicting packages...

Package (1)             Old Version  New Version  Net Change  Download Size

core/archlinux-keyring  20230225-1   20230225-1     0.00 MiB       1.13 MiB

Total Download Size:   1.13 MiB
Total Installed Size:  1.60 MiB
Net Upgrade Size:      0.00 MiB

:: Proceed with installation? [Y/n]
:: Retrieving packages...
 archlinux-keyring-20230225-1-any downloading...
checking keyring...
checking package integrity...
loading package files...
checking for file conflicts...
:: Processing package changes...
reinstalling archlinux-keyring...
could not change the root directory (Operation not permitted)
error: command failed to execute correctly
could not change the root directory (Operation not permitted)
error: command failed to execute correctly
:: Running post-transaction hooks...
(1/2) Reloading system manager configuration...
could not change the root directory (Operation not permitted)
error: command failed to execute correctly
(2/2) Arming ConditionNeedsUpdate...
could not change the root directory (Operation not permitted)
error: command failed to execute correctly
:: Synchronizing package databases...
 core downloading...
 extra downloading...
 community downloading...
:: Starting full system upgrade...
[..]
```

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected
Container operations to finish without issues.

```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting.

I've tried the podman version 4.3.x and it works fine so it's something happening in 4.4.x releases.

comments.body: @Luap99 This seems to work. Is this something that I need to do from now on?
Maybe unless we revert the default caps, I close it as dup of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17504
